document: Introduction to Internet of Things Assignment-Week 2 Solutions

questions.questionText: The full form of MQTT is
questions.explanation: The full form of MQTT is Message Queue Telemetry Transport. See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 01:51
XMPP uses the client-server architecture. See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 12:03

questions.questionText: With respect to AMQP, which of the following message delivery guarantees allow each message to be delivered certainly but also may be delivered multiple times.
questions.explanation: The message delivery guarantees of AMQP are - At-most-once- each message is delivered once or never. At-least-once each message is certain to be delivered, but may do so multiple times. Exactly-once - message will always certainly arrive and do so only once. See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 05:35

questions.questionText: In MQTT for IoT, the Publishers are
questions.explanation: In MQTT, the Publishers are lightweight sensors. See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 04:49

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT a method in MQTT?
questions.explanation: Following are the methods in MQTT - a. Connect b. Disconnect c. Subscribe d. Unsubscribe e. Publish. See lecture 6 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part II) @ 06:18
CoAP has four modes for messaging. See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 04:33

questions.questionText: State True or False CoAP architecture is divided into three sub-layers. The messaging sub-layer is responsible for reliability and duplication of messages, while the request-response sub-layer is responsible for communication. The last layer, which is optional, is responsible for user registration.
questions.explanation: CoAP architecture is divided into two sub-layers. The messaging sub-layer is responsible for reliability and duplication of messages, while the request-response sub-layer is responsible for communication. See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 05:31

questions.questionText: What is the full form of XMPP in IoT?
options: b. Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol
questions.explanation: XMPP stands for Extensible Messaging and Presence Protocol. See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 11:58

questions.questionText: State whether the following statement if True or False. Statement: XMPP is not an open standard protocol.
questions.explanation: XMPP is an open standard protocol. See lecture 7 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part III) @ 11:58

questions.questionText: What is the full is/are not frame type of the AMQP protocol?
questions.explanation: None of these is a valid AMQP frame type. See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking Part IV) 07:07

questions.questionText: What is the basic unit of data in the AMQP protocol?
questions.explanation: In AMQP, the basic unit of data is called a frame. See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 0:41

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are exchange types in AMQP?
questions.explanation: The AMQP exchange types are a) Direct b) Fan-out c) Topic d) Header. See lecture 8 (Basics of IoT Networking - Part IV) @ 08:56

questions.questionText: Which of the following is/are not an application of 6LoWPAN?
questions.explanation: Both are areas where 6LoWPAN can be applied. See lecture 10 (Connectivity Technologies - Part-II) @ 04:32

The OSI model is a conceptual framework that divides any networked communication system into seven layers. See Page number 10, Chapter 1, Book Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition 1 (2021)

questions.questionText: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents which of the following?
options: a. The destination node address of the packet
b. The intermediate hop in the network
questions.explanation: The "Destination Address" in the IPv4 packet represents the address of the destination node in the network. See Page number - 18, Chapter 1, Book - Introduction to IoT, Authors - Sudip Misra, Anandarup Mukherjee, and Arijit Roy, Publisher - Cambridge University Press, Edition - 1 (2021)
